name: IExecutionStrategy
description: Contract for workflow execution strategies. Strategies control how a workflow's steps are iterated and executed. The default SequentialStrategy runs steps in declaration order; future strategies (parallel, goal-oriented, reflection) can be plugged in without modifying the executor core.

methods.name: execute
methods.description: Execute the workflow steps using this strategy. The strategy iterates the workflow's steps and calls step_runner(step, context) for each one. Results are stored in context.step_results as a side-effect of the callback.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
The strategy only decides which steps to run and in what order — per-step concerns (condition evaluation, timeout, error strategies, lifecycle hooks) are handled by the step_runner callback
Results must be stored in context.step_results via the step_runner callback — the strategy itself returns void
execute() must call step_runner for each step it decides to run
The strategy must respect context.suspended state
